title: LayerKey model for the local cache
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
This changes the algorithm to determine if the next build command is already in the local store. Instead of enumerating the layers one by one until it finds a hit, the new approach calculates a key that represents the layer. This will allow the system to be extended with a distributed cache. 

#### How to verify it

None of the existing tests should change. New unit tests were added, they should not fail as well.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

No

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
None

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *george-harness*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Looks pretty reasonable.  I don't understand why the new `cache` subpackage introduces an interface that we don't use anywhere, and which doesn't include the `PopulateLayer` method that the `StageExecutor` depends on.  Callers can't pass us one, so what's it for?

The change is just a phase 1. The LayerProvider is just there for the next step where I am going to add more implementations for it. I plan to add:
CascadeLayerProvider, which will combine multiple LayerProvider in a cascade fashion ... to eliminate the need of reaching to distributed cache if the layer is already in the local cache, and similar.
FSLayerProvider to allow for unit testing
RegistryRepoLayerProvider to allow for loading and storing layers to a registry in a particular repo.
S3LayerProvider to allow for loading and storing layers to s3 object storage
@george-harness quick review LGTM, but it looks like you need a rebase at this point.
